title: GeoDE: A Geographically Diverse Evaluation Dataset for Object Recognition
topic_group: GeoDE and geographically diverse object recognition
main_finding: Introduces GeoDE as a geographically diverse object-recognition evaluation dataset
limitation: Dataset-specific local statistics must be audited separately
how_it_supports_this_paper: Core dataset motivation and direct support for region-level evaluation

title: The Dollar Street Dataset: Images Representing the Geographic and Socioeconomic Diversity of the World
topic_group: Dollar Street and socioeconomic/geographic visual diversity
main_finding: Introduces Dollar Street as a household-object dataset with geographic and socioeconomic diversity
limitation: Local subset statistics must be audited separately
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports dataset choice and socioeconomic/geographic diversity motivation

title: Does Object Recognition Work for Everyone?
topic_group: Geographic and regional bias in computer vision
method: empirical evaluation across countries and income
main_finding: Reports object-recognition disparities across countries and income contexts on Dollar Street
limitation: Different models and setup from this paper
how_it_supports_this_paper: Closest prior motivation for regional/socioeconomic object-recognition disparity

title: No Classification without Representation
main_finding: Finds amerocentric/eurocentric representation bias and locale-linked performance differences
limitation: Older and not specific to GeoDE/Dollar Street

title: GeoNet: Benchmarking Unsupervised Adaptation across Geographies

title: Unbiased Look at Dataset Bias
main_finding: Shows dataset-specific biases can affect recognition generalization
